title: VPN for Remote Work: What Your Company Should Know in 2026
description: Remote workers handling sensitive company data need a VPN. Here is what actually matters for business use and what consumer VPNs miss.
content:
Why Remote Work and VPNs Go Together

When employees work from home or from public networks, their internet traffic is exposed by default. A VPN encrypts that traffic and routes it through a secure server, making it unreadable to anyone on the same network. For employees handling customer data, financial records, or proprietary systems, this is not optional.

Consumer VPN vs Business VPN

Consumer VPNs (NordVPN, ExpressVPN, Mullvad) are designed for individual privacy. Business VPNs (Cisco AnyConnect, Palo Alto GlobalProtect, Perimeter 81, NordLayer) are designed for managed, policy-controlled access to company resources. The key differences are: centralized user management, forced enrollment, split tunneling controls, and audit logging. If your company has more than five remote employees, a consumer VPN is the wrong tool.

Split Tunneling for Remote Workers

Split tunneling allows only traffic to specific company resources to go through the VPN, while general internet traffic goes directly to the internet. This reduces latency for video calls and general browsing while keeping company data protected. Most business VPN solutions support split tunneling and allow IT to define which destinations go through the tunnel.

Zero Trust vs Traditional VPN

The security industry is moving toward Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA), which grants access per-application rather than per-network. Solutions like Cloudflare Access, Zscaler Private Access, and Google BeyondCorp implement this model. ZTNA is more granular and more secure than traditional VPN tunnels, but requires more configuration. For small teams, a managed business VPN is still the practical choice. For larger organizations with complex access needs, ZTNA is worth evaluating.

Practical Recommendations

For teams of 1-10 remote workers: NordLayer or Perimeter 81 on the entry tier. For 10-100 employees: evaluate Cloudflare Teams or a full ZTNA solution. For employees who just need to access a specific internal tool: Tailscale is a lightweight mesh VPN that is easy to deploy and does not require managing a VPN server. Avoid asking employees to use personal consumer VPNs for work traffic.
